body:
# Minimal Node.js HTTP server returning "Hello, World!" on GET /

## Context

We need a minimal HTTP server implemented in plain Node.js — ESM modules, no
external web framework (no Express/Fastify/Koa) — built entirely on the
built-in `node:http` module. This is a self-contained deliverable: a single
source file plus a `package.json`.

Behaviour contract:

- The server binds to the port given by the `PORT` environment variable,
  defaulting to `3000` when `PORT` is unset.
- A `GET` request to path `/` returns HTTP `200` with a `Content-Type` header
  of `text/html` and a response body containing the exact text
  `Hello, World!`.
- The project exposes an npm `start` script that launches the server.

The implementation must stay small and dependency-free: one source file
(e.g. `server.mjs` or `src/server.js` with `"type": "module"`) and
`package.json`, nothing more.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `package.json` exists with `"type": "module"` and a `"start"` script that
      launches the single server source file via `node`.
- [ ] The server is implemented in a single ESM source file using only the
      built-in `node:http` module — no external web-framework dependency is
      added to `package.json`.
- [ ] The server listens on `process.env.PORT` when set, and on `3000` when
      `PORT` is unset.
- [ ] A `GET` request to `/` responds with status `200`.
- [ ] The response to `GET /` sets `Content-Type: text/html`.
- [ ] The response body for `GET /` contains the exact text `Hello, World!`.
- [ ] `npm start` boots the server without error and it accepts connections on
      the resolved port.

## Out of Scope

- Any external web framework or HTTP library (Express, Fastify, Koa, etc.).
- Routing beyond `GET /` (other paths/methods may return a default response but
  are not specified here).
- TLS/HTTPS, HTTP/2, clustering, or process managers (PM2, systemd).
- Request logging, metrics, health-check endpoints, or graceful-shutdown
  handling beyond what a minimal server needs.
- Dockerfiles, CI pipelines, or deployment configuration.
- A test framework/dependency — verification is done by exercising the running
  server (see Notes).

## Notes

- Suggested layout: a single `server.mjs` (or `src/server.js`) plus
  `package.json` with `"type": "module"` and `"scripts": { "start": "node
  server.mjs" }`.
- Resolve the port as `const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;` and pass it to
  `server.listen(PORT)`.
- Use `http.createServer((req, res) => …)`; for `GET /` write
  `res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' })` then
  `res.end('Hello, World!')` (the body may be wrapped in minimal HTML as long
  as it contains the exact substring `Hello, World!`).
- Verification (no test dependency required): start the server with
  `npm start`, then `curl -i http://localhost:3000/` and confirm the `200`
  status, the `Content-Type: text/html` header, and the `Hello, World!` body;
  repeat with `PORT=4000 npm start` to confirm the env-var override.
- Standalone Story — no parent Epic. Next step after planning:
  `/deliver <storyId>`.
title: Minimal Node.js HTTP server returning "Hello, World!" on GET /
